vulnerability_analysis.summary: OLLAMA_TOKEN (9e34679...) was hardcoded in systemd unit file /workspaces/Jit/jit-heartbeat.service, exposing production API credentials in a world-readable configuration file.

vulnerability_details.exposure_vector: Unit file readable by all system users via systemctl show, grep, cat, or process inspection
vulnerability_details.credential_scope: Production API access to https://ollama.mdes-innova.online (Thai language model serving)
vulnerability_details.git_history_status: Token present in at least one commit (memory/heartbeats/beat-1.md shows 9e34679 in curl command)

attack_scenarios.attacker: Any user with shell access to server
attack_scenarios.impact: Run expensive model inferences at victim's quota, DoS Thai language processing

attack_scenarios.attacker: Log aggregation tools, systemd journal readers
attack_scenarios.impact: Token exposed in centralized logging systems if journalctl output is shared

attack_scenarios.attacker: DevOps tooling, APM agents, shell history
attack_scenarios.impact: Credential in monitoring dashboards or shell history files

attack_scenarios.attacker: Git repository cloners, CI/CD logs, backups
attack_scenarios.impact: Historical access to token even after removal from current version

current_state_assessment.reason_git: beat-1.md contains curl command with inline 9e34679... token in error output
current_state_assessment.reason_codebase: grep -r '9e34679' shows only in specs, TOR, and memory logs

fix_design.approach: Hybrid: Use systemd LoadCredential (primary) + fallback to EnvironmentFile (already in place)
fix_design.rationale: LoadCredential is systemd native (v247+), isolates credentials from process env, supports rotation. EnvironmentFile already implemented as bridge.

architecture_layers.mechanism: systemd LoadCredential directive points to credentials directory
LoadCredential exposes token via $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY/ollama_token (not in process env)

architecture_layers.target_state: Read OLLAMA_TOKEN from $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY/ollama_token OR fallback to $OLLAMA_TOKEN env var
architecture_layers.mechanism: Graceful fallback: if CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY defined, source from there; else use inherited env

architecture_layers.current_state: Token visible in memory/heartbeats/beat-1.md error output
architecture_layers.target_state: Scrub git history using BFG or git-filter-repo before forcing credentials policy
Disable old token at MDES; issue new token; store in credentials file
architecture_layers.mechanism: Coordinate with MDES team; test new token before disabling old

systemd_unit_fix.after:
[Service]
# LoadCredential: systemd v247+ reads from /etc/jit-credentials/ and exposes via $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY
LoadCredential=ollama_token:/etc/jit-credentials/ollama_token
ExecStart=/bin/bash -c 'export OLLAMA_TOKEN=$(cat $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY/ollama_token 2>/dev/null || echo "") && bash scripts/heartbeat-24h-daemon.sh'

script_fix.before: # No explicit credential source; relies on inherited OLLAMA_TOKEN from systemd Environment=
script_fix.after:
# Secure credential loading: prefer systemd LoadCredential, fallback to EnvironmentFile
if [[ -n "${CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY:-}" && -f "$CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY/ollama_token" ]]; then
  export OLLAMA_TOKEN=$(cat "$CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY/ollama_token")
fi
# OLLAMA_TOKEN is now either from LoadCredential or EnvironmentFile, never hardcoded

logic: 2. If running with sudo: create /etc/jit-credentials with mode 700, owned by root:root
3. If not sudo: use ~/.jit-credentials with mode 700 (user-local)
4. Prompt for new OLLAMA_TOKEN (from MDES dashboard after rotation)
5. Write token to credentials_dir/ollama_token with mode 600
6. Validate: systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl restart jit-heartbeat

git_history_scrub.post_scrub: git push --force origin main (after team coordination)

steps: 1. Generate new token in MDES Ollama dashboard
2. Store new token in credentials file (setup-credentials.sh)
5. Monitor logs: journalctl -u jit-heartbeat -f (watch for successful API calls)
6. Once verified: disable old token in MDES dashboard

files_to_modify.description: Replace EnvironmentFile with LoadCredential for native systemd credential handling. LoadCredential exposes token via $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY, preventing it from entering process environment.
Keep ops/ and systemd/ in sync. Clarify which is canonical (likely ops/).

files_to_modify.current_state: Reads OLLAMA_TOKEN from inherited environment; no explicit credential source handling
files_to_modify.target_state: Add explicit credential loading: check $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY first, fallback to env var
files_to_modify.description: Make credential source explicit and testable. If CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY is set (systemd LoadCredential), read token from there. Otherwise use inherited OLLAMA_TOKEN.

files_to_modify.target_state: Interactive setup script for credentials directory
files_to_modify.description: Creates /etc/jit-credentials (prod, requires sudo) or ~/.jit-credentials (dev). Guides user to store new OLLAMA_TOKEN. Validates systemd can read it. Runs validation tests.

files_to_modify.current_state: Loads .env file with `source`, which may contain token in plaintext
files_to_modify.target_state: Migrate to read OLLAMA_TOKEN from credentials file or environment (not hardcoded .env)
files_to_modify.description: If llm.sh directly sources .env with OLLAMA_TOKEN, add credential loading logic here too. Verify llm.sh can work with LoadCredential-supplied token.

files_to_modify.current_state: Contains curl error with inline 9e34679 token
files_to_modify.description: Replace visible token with [REDACTED] in curl command. This is the source of token leakage in git history.

files_to_modify.current_state: No mention of credential management procedure
files_to_modify.description: Document how agents access OLLAMA_TOKEN securely. Reference setup-credentials.sh, explain LoadCredential vs EnvironmentFile.

files_to_modify.current_state: May not document credential setup for production
files_to_modify.target_state: Add 'Credentials Setup' section with step-by-step instructions
files_to_modify.description: Production deployment guide: how to create /etc/jit-credentials, load token, validate, rotate.

files_to_modify.target_state: Keep for dev/test, mark as deprecated in favor of LoadCredential
files_to_modify.description: Can remain for local development (EnvironmentFile still works). In production, credentials come from /etc/jit-credentials via LoadCredential.
If bootstrap is used to initialize production, add call to setup-credentials.sh or prompt for token input.

tests_needed.name: Service loads without hardcoded token
tests_needed.command: grep -i 'Environment.*OLLAMA_TOKEN' /workspaces/Jit/systemd/jit-heartbeat.service && echo 'FAIL: Token found in unit file' || echo 'PASS: No token in unit file'
tests_needed.expected_result: PASS: No token in unit file
tests_needed.acceptance: Token must not appear in Environment= directive

tests_needed.name: LoadCredential directive is present
tests_needed.acceptance: LoadCredential directive must reference the credentials file

tests_needed.name: Credentials file has correct permissions
tests_needed.acceptance: File must be readable only by owner (mode 0600)

tests_needed.name: Credentials file is owned by root
tests_needed.acceptance: Credentials must be owned by root:root to prevent tampering

tests_needed.name: Service starts without errors
tests_needed.command: systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl start jit-heartbeat && sleep 2 && systemctl is-active jit-heartbeat
tests_needed.acceptance: Service must enter active state after start

tests_needed.name: Token does not appear in process environment
tests_needed.command: systemctl show jit-heartbeat.service -p Environment | grep -i OLLAMA_TOKEN
tests_needed.acceptance: systemctl show must not reveal OLLAMA_TOKEN in process environment

tests_needed.name: Token does not appear in ps output
tests_needed.command: ps aux | grep heartbeat | grep -v grep | grep -c OLLAMA_TOKEN
tests_needed.acceptance: ps output must not contain visible token

tests_needed.name: Service can reach Ollama API
tests_needed.expected_result: Must contain successful API call logs
tests_needed.acceptance: Service logs must show successful Ollama API communication

tests_needed.name: Git history does not contain token
tests_needed.acceptance: git log must return zero matches for token string

tests_needed.name: No token in codebase grep
tests_needed.acceptance: Token must not appear outside .git and .secrets directories

tests_needed.name: Heartbeat script reads credential correctly
tests_needed.expected_result: Script must execute without credential errors
tests_needed.acceptance: Script must not fail due to missing OLLAMA_TOKEN (even if Ollama is unavailable)

tests_needed.name: setup-credentials.sh is executable
tests_needed.acceptance: setup-credentials.sh must be created and executable

tests_needed.name: Token rotation does not break service
tests_needed.command: echo 'NEW_TOKEN_12345' | sudo tee /etc/jit-credentials/ollama_token > /dev/null && systemctl restart jit-heartbeat && sleep 2 && systemctl is-active jit-heartbeat
tests_needed.acceptance: Service must restart cleanly with new token

tests_needed.name: Credentials directory permissions prevent unauthorized access
tests_needed.acceptance: Directory must be accessible only by owner (mode 0700)

tests_needed.name: Documentation updated with credential procedures
tests_needed.acceptance: Both CLAUDE.md and deployment-guide.md must reference credential setup

step_1_update_service_file.to:
[Service]
# Credentials: systemd v247+ LoadCredential exposes token via $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY
LoadCredential=ollama_token:/etc/jit-credentials/ollama_token
step_1_update_service_file.comments: LoadCredential is safer than EnvironmentFile: token never enters process environment

step_2_update_exec_start.action: Update ExecStart to source token from $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY
step_2_update_exec_start.comments: Make credential source explicit. Silent fallback to empty string if file doesn't exist (for dev/testing).

step_3_create_setup_script.action: Create interactive setup script for credentials
pseudocode: # Check if running with sudo (prod) or user (dev)
if curl -s https://ollama.mdes-innova.online/api/health -H "Authorization: Bearer $token" | grep -q ok; then
echo '⚠️  Token validation failed (Ollama may be unreachable)'

code: # Secure credential loading: prefer systemd LoadCredential, fallback to inherited env
log_beat "INFO" "✅ Loaded OLLAMA_TOKEN from systemd LoadCredential"
log_beat "WARN" "⚠️  OLLAMA_TOKEN not set (will fail if Ollama API is called)"

step_5_redact_git_history.notes: Then use git-filter-repo to scrub full history

step_6_scrub_git_history.post_action: git push --force origin main (after team coordination)
step_6_scrub_git_history.notes: Verify no token remains: git log -S '9e34679' (should return empty)

step_7_rotate_token.action: Coordinate with MDES to disable old token and issue new one
steps: 1. Contact MDES Ollama team (via shared Slack/email)
2. Request new OLLAMA_TOKEN for innova agent
6. Monitor logs: journalctl -u jit-heartbeat -f (watch for successful API calls)
7. Disable old token: Instruct MDES to revoke 9e34679...

sections: Link to security audit (DEVOPS-001) and related tickets

pre_deployment_checks: Verify systemd version: systemctl --version | head -1 (must be v247+)
Confirm sudo access: sudo -n systemctl status (no password prompt)
Check git permissions: git branch -a | grep main
deployment_verification: systemctl status jit-heartbeat (should show active or inactive; not failed)
systemctl show jit-heartbeat.service -p Environment | grep -q OLLAMA_TOKEN || echo 'PASS: No token in environment'
ps aux | grep heartbeat | grep -v grep | grep OLLAMA_TOKEN || echo 'PASS: No token visible in process'
journalctl -u jit-heartbeat -n 20 (should show successful heartbeat or Ollama API calls)
post_deployment_monitoring: Verify Ollama API calls succeed (no auth errors)
Check for service restarts due to failures
Monitor for token-related errors in journal

if_LoadCredential_not_supported: Revert to EnvironmentFile: systemctl edit jit-heartbeat
Create /etc/jit/ollama.env with proper permissions (mode 0600, owned by root)
if_token_rotation_fails: Keep old token available during transition
Retry new token once MDES confirms setup
if_git_scrub_causes_issues: Recover from reflog: git checkout $(git reflog | grep 'reset:' | head -1)
Keep file-level credentials clean (don't re-add token)

risk_assessment.risk: systemd version does not support LoadCredential (< v247)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Pre-check systemd version; fallback to EnvironmentFile. Ubuntu 21.04+ has v248+.

risk_assessment.risk: Service fails to read token from $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY
risk_assessment.mitigation: Test in dev environment first; add error handling in script with graceful fallback

risk_assessment.risk: Git scrub breaks team workflow or loses commits
risk_assessment.mitigation: Communicate force-push to team; use git-filter-repo (safer than BFG); keep reflog for recovery

risk_assessment.risk: Token rotation creates auth errors (MDES delays)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Have both old and new tokens available during transition; test new token before disabling old

risk_assessment.risk: Credentials file accidentally deleted or permissions changed
risk_assessment.mitigation: Document setup procedure; add to bootstrap.sh; use setup-credentials.sh to recreate
Ensure logs don't echo token; monitor journalctl output; consider log filtering rules

effort_summary.status: ESTIMATED (spec allocation is 3h; actual implementation ~3.5h with all steps)
